content: Existing Self-Refine frameworks can be significantly upgraded by integrating multiple inconsistent perspective sampling from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to break the blind spot of single-perspective iterative feedback. This creates a self-improving loop where the LLM first cross-checks its initial output against conflicting solution perspectives from its own parameters, then feeds the identified inconsistencies into Self-Refine's iterative refinement pipeline without any additional training or external supervision.
